  1. Home
  2. › Diet & Nutrition
  3. › High-Protein

High-Protein Diet for Dogs

High-protein diets contain elevated levels of animal-based proteins, typically 30% or more on a dry matter basis. Designed for active, working, or athletic dogs with high energy demands.

✓ Benefits

  • Supports lean muscle mass
  • Provides sustained energy for active dogs
  • Can aid muscle recovery after exercise
  • Often highly palatable

⚠ Risks & Considerations

  • May worsen kidney disease in dogs with pre-existing conditions
  • Not appropriate for sedentary or overweight dogs
  • Can cause loose stools if introduced too quickly

Who Is This Diet Suitable For?

working dogs sporting breeds highly active dogs dogs recovering from illness or surgery

Feeding Guidelines by Dog Size

These are general guidelines for the High-Protein diet. Always adjust portions based on your dog's individual weight, activity level, and life stage. Consult your vet before making significant changes to your dog's diet.

Size Weight Range Cups Per Day Feeding Frequency
Small up to 10 kg 0.5-1 cups 2 meals
Medium 10–25 kg 1-2 cups 2 meals
Large 25–45 kg 2-3.5 cups 2 meals
Giant 45 kg+ 3.5-5 cups 2-3 meals

Cups are a rough guide based on standard 8 fl oz measuring cups. Always follow the manufacturer's label for calorie-dense or prescription formulas.

Recommended Brands

The following brands are well-regarded within the high-protein category. Look for products where a named protein source appears as the first ingredient.

  • Orijen
  • Acana
  • Merrick Backcountry
  • Zignature

Top Picks: High-Protein Dog Food

Dog Food

High-Protein Dog Food — Top Pick

A leading high-protein formula with high-quality protein as the first ingredient. Suitable for adult dogs across all sizes. Supports lean muscle mass.

Dog Food

High-Protein Wet Food for Dogs

A wet high-protein option for dogs that prefer a softer texture or need extra hydration. Particularly suitable for working dogs.

Check Price on Amazon

As an Amazon Associate we earn from qualifying purchases.

Supplement

Digestive Support Supplement

Probiotics and digestive enzymes to complement a high-protein diet. Helps maintain gut balance, especially during dietary transitions. Helps offset: may worsen kidney disease in dogs with pre-existing conditions.

Check Price on Amazon

As an Amazon Associate we earn from qualifying purchases.

Breeds That Benefit From a High-Protein Diet

The High-Protein diet is particularly well-suited to these breeds. Click any breed to view their full diet and nutrition guide.

  • Labrador Retriever
  • Golden Retriever
  • German Shepherd
  • Poodle
  • Beagle
  • Rottweiler
  • German Shorthaired Pointer
